title: Corymborkis densiflora
intro: Corymborkis densiflora, also known as the orchid of Madagascar, is a rare and endangered epiphytic orchid native to the island of Madagascar. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Corymborkis densiflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal medicine, Corymborkis densiflora is commonly prepared as a tincture or dried extract, and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
